leakDetected: Methodology excerpt: clean (no crown mention). Harness API does name crown as controlComp's fixed B2 slot — a structural fixture fact only, no kit magnitudes/dispositions/answers, so the review proceeds from prose. Flagging for completeness.

spec.scope: Generic ATK stat, but FLAT-ADD semantics: '% of the skill user's ATK' = casterAtkPct (0.6451 × crown's own ATK added flat), NOT atkPct scaling of each target's own ATK
spec.durationSemantics: 15 s wall-clock, re-applied at every Full Burst entry (~75% uptime on a ~20 s rotation)
spec.triggerIdentity: fullBurstEnter (start of ANY team Full Burst) — not burstCast; fires every rotation regardless of which B3 completed the chain
spec.targetSet: Only allies who cast their Burst Skill in the rotation leading into this FB (the rotationCasters set), self included — crown casts B2 every rotation so she is always in it
spec.nearestWrongModel: atkPct 64.51 applied to ALL allies — double misread: own-ATK scaling instead of caster-flat-add (over-credits high-ATK carries massively), and no caster/non-caster partition (over-credits the sitting-out B3)
spec.distinguishingAssertion: In controlComp(carry, helm=true): at each fullBurstStart a buffApply{stat:'casterAtkPct', value:64.51} lands on liter, crown, and the casting carry but NEVER on helm (the non-casting second B3); the carry's effectiveAtk delta equals 0.6451 × crown's staticAtk exactly, independent of the carry's own base ATK
spec.inertness: helm's ATK must not move; no application before the first Full Burst; a target's own ATK magnitude must not change the buff's absolute size

spec.scope: Weapon-state modifier (reloadSpeedPct) — gates shots fired, this IS damage (taxonomy #6); applies to the caster branch's units
spec.triggerIdentity: fullBurstEnter, same block as the ATK line
spec.nearestWrongModel: Skipped as 'defensive QoL / no damage' — the classic reload-speed drop; or given durationSec 10 (clipped to the FB window)
spec.distinguishingAssertion: reload events starting inside the 15 s window complete in reloadFrames/1.4435 (crown: 171 → ~118 frames); withPatchedOverride removing only this line produces strictly fewer total shot events for crown and the carry over 180 s
spec.inertness: reload duration outside the 15 s window is unmodified base

spec.scope: Caster-DEF-scaled flat DEF grant; no caster-DEF StatKey exists and self/ally DEF is damage-inert in v1 (no HP pool, boss deals no damage)
spec.targetSet: Allies who did NOT cast their Burst Skill this rotation
spec.nearestWrongModel: Dropping the whole non-caster BLOCK because its lead line is defensive — taking the sibling Reload Speed line (next entry) down with it
spec.distinguishingAssertion: withPatchedOverride removing the DEF line alone → totals byte-identical (inertness proof); the line lives in unmodeled/note, not as a damage-relevant stat
spec.inertness: Zero damage contribution under any comp

spec.scope: Weapon-state (reloadSpeedPct) for the NON-casting partition — net effect of the two branches: every ally gets reload speed at FB, only casters get ATK
spec.targetSet: Allies who did not cast this rotation (helm in the control comp; empty set when every unit bursts)
spec.nearestWrongModel: Branch dropped — reload speed only reaching burst-casters, starving the sitting-out B3 (helm) of ~44% reload uptime
spec.distinguishingAssertion: helm receives buffApply{stat:'reloadSpeedPct', value:44.35} at every fullBurstStart despite never casting, while receiving NO casterAtkPct buff — both facts in the same event window
spec.inertness: helm must never receive the 64.51% ATK branch

spec.scope: Counter counts crown's own normal-attack ROUNDS (MG hitsPerShot 1 → rounds = pulls = ammo spent); the Incoming-healing ▲4.06% payload itself has no engine consumer → unmodeled verbatim, but the COUNTER is the load-bearing timer of the whole S2 chain
spec.durationSemantics: Stacks, 'continuously' = no time expiry; removed only by the max-stack consumption. Full cycle = 43 × 20 = 860 rounds
spec.triggerIdentity: hitCount count:43 (rounds, spanning reloads), self-scoped stack/resource accrual
spec.nearestWrongModel: One stack per attack, or 20 stacks granted at the 43rd attack — collapsing the 860-round cycle to 43 rounds (~20× chain-frequency over-credit); or counting trigger pulls ≠ rounds on a future hitsPerShot>1 weapon
spec.distinguishingAssertion: Consecutive team attackDamagePct-20.99 applications (line below) are separated by exactly 860 crown shot events in the event log — never 43
spec.inertness: The 4.06% incoming-healing magnitude must move zero damage; stack accrual pauses whenever crown is not firing (wind-up, reload, stun)

spec.scope: Self; invulnerability + taunt are damage-inert (boss deals no damage, partless, targeting irrelevant); the HP restore's MAGNITUDE (5.23% own final Max HP) is inert but its EVENT is the trigger feeding the team buff — tandem trap #4, do not skip
spec.durationSemantics: Stacks reset to 0 on activation; invuln/taunt 5 s (inert); heal instant
spec.triggerIdentity: Threshold: Relax == 20 at accrual time → consume-and-fire (resource gate + reset), immediately chaining into the recovery block
spec.nearestWrongModel: Invulnerability modeled as a firing interruption (it is not — she keeps attacking), or the heal event silently dropped as 'defensive' so the downstream 20.99% team buff NEVER fires
spec.distinguishingAssertion: crown's shot cadence is unbroken across the consumption frame (no gap beyond normal MG cycle), AND the team Attack-Damage buffApply of the next line fires at this same frame
spec.inertness: No direct damage, no shot-count change, no gauge change from invuln/taunt/heal themselves

spec.scope: 'Attack Damage' = the Damage-Up bucket (attackDamagePct), applying to ALL of each ally's damage — not normal-attacks-only
spec.triggerIdentity: Fires when HER OWN S2 recovery lands — a deterministic self-contained chain, so an encoding as hitCount count:860 (43×20) is behaviorally equivalent and acceptable; it must NOT be keyed to arbitrary team heals or to an invented interval
spec.nearestWrongModel: Keyed to the 43-hit stack trigger instead of the 860-round max-stack heal — ~20× uptime over-credit; second-nearest: keyed to any teammate's heal, over-firing whenever a healer is in comp
spec.distinguishingAssertion: First buffApply{stat:'attackDamagePct', value:20.99, 7s} to all 5 units occurs only after crown's 860th round (mid-fight, tens of seconds in — never at t=0), and inter-application spacing equals the 860-round accrual time including reloads/wind-up
spec.inertness: Never fires at t=0; frequency depends only on crown's own fire cadence, not on team composition or external heals

spec.scope: Damage-Up bucket (attackDamagePct), all of each ally's damage
spec.durationSemantics: 15 s wall-clock — NOT clipped to the 10 s FB window; from a pre-FB cast it covers the whole FB plus a ~5 s tail
spec.triggerIdentity: burstCast — crown's OWN B2 cast (lands pre-FB, before the +50% window opens). With cd 20 s she casts every rotation in a standard comp, so burstCast and fullBurstEnter coincide there — the misread only surfaces with a competing B2
spec.nearestWrongModel: fullBurstEnter-keyed (fires even on rotations where a DIFFERENT B2 casts — over-credit in dual-B2 comps) and/or durationSec 10
spec.distinguishingAssertion: buffApply lands at crown's burstCast event frame, strictly BEFORE the same rotation's fullBurstStart, and buffRemove falls ~5 s after fullBurstEnd; in a comp where another B2 takes the slot, zero applications
spec.inertness: Exactly one application per crown burst — no double-fire at FB entry

spec.scope: Shield EVENT channel only — no HP pool modeled, zero direct damage; maxHpPct scales off CROWN's final Max HP (own-HP rule #7, ally HP grants don't feed it)
spec.triggerIdentity: burstCast (same cast as the Attack-Damage line)
spec.nearestWrongModel: Skipped as 'defensive, no damage' — which silences every teammate's `shielded` trigger and `requiresShielded` gate (naga-style shield-synergy tandem, taxonomy #4)
spec.distinguishingAssertion: Each crown burst emits a shield effect to all 5 units (a shield-synergy ally's `shielded` trigger fires per crown burst); with no shield-consumer in comp, totals are unchanged when the line is removed via withPatchedOverride
spec.inertness: Must add zero damage itself; the shield's HP magnitude must not feed any ally's atkOfMaxHpPct

skill1: DEF ▲ 37.44% of the skill user's DEF for 15 sec
skill2: Relax: Incoming healing ▲ 4.06% continuously (stat magnitude — counter itself IS modeled)
Attract: Taunts all enemies for 5 sec
Restores HP equal to 5.23% of the skill user's final Max HP (magnitude only — the recovery EVENT must remain as the team-buff trigger)

notes: crown's kit deals ZERO direct damage — everything is buffs/shield, so a global inertness sweep applies: no kit-sourced damage/dot events with srcSlot=crown may exist. Expected shared-prior misreads to hunt: (1) skill1 caster/non-caster PARTITION flattened to 'all allies' — the single biggest over-credit, since it hands the 64.51% caster-ATK flat add to a non-casting B3; (2) casterAtkPct vs atkPct — 'of the skill user's ATK' is a flat add of crown's ATK, and crown is a Defender with modest ATK, so own-ATK scaling on a carry inflates hugely; (3) the S2 chain collapsed — team 20.99% keyed to every 43 hits instead of the 860-round max-stack heal, or S2 skipped wholesale because its surface reads defensive; (4) reload-speed lines dropped as QoL (weapon-state = damage); (5) burst keyed fullBurstEnter — invisible in the control comp (crown is the sole B2 and casts every rotation) so the test MUST assert the buffApply frame precedes fullBurstStart, and ideally probe a dual-B2 comp. Fixture note: crown IS controlComp's fixed B2, so controlComp(carry, helm=true) is the natural rig — helm is a non-casting B3 every rotation and cleanly probes the skill1 partition (reload+DEF branch yes, ATK branch never). Timing note: assert the S2 cycle by EVENT COUNT (860 shot events between team-buff applications), never by wall-clock, so MG wind-up/reload/reload-speed-buff interactions can't flake the test — and note the skill1/burst reload-speed buffs themselves shorten the 860-round accrual time (a correct interaction, not a bug).
